text:
It was a cold Tuesday morning, and I woke up feeling a little under the weather. The usual caffeine jolt didn't do the job. Instead of rushing out the door, deciding I'd catch this sickliness later, I chose to pull on a cozy, old-faded jacket over my pajamas and make a warm tea.

The city was quiet around this hour, just a few coffee shops hustling through their caffeine routines. I sat at my usual spot at the corner café, the aroma of freshly brewed coffee and pastries warming me. I reached for my book and started to read, letting the rhythmic sound of the espresso machine drown out the hum of the city.

As I flipped through pages, a quiet figure slipped across the sidewalk. They passed by unnoticed until I started to notice the bags slung across their shoulders.
